sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis for pyridine was performed using the `chemkit` tool.

**Fukui Reactivity Indices** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, density-fit) in PySCF, **gas phase**, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: built from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz`; this was a single point calculation on the fixed geometry. SCF converged.

The most electrophilic site is the Nitrogen atom (atom index 0, label 1) with an f+ value of **0.48422572**.
The most nucleophilic site is a Carbon atom (atom index 3, label 4) with an f- value of **0.1182626**.

The full Fukui indices are:
f+: `[0.48422572, 0.32447226, 0.14032093, 0.14039586, 0.29962626, 0.29947707, -0.1415132, -0.1336841, -0.13365905, -0.1398163, -0.13984545]`
f-: `[0.064122671, 0.0061877146, 0.11823139, 0.1182626, 0.11206595, 0.11200985, 0.08284851, 0.098179487, 0.09818226, 0.094960093, 0.094949479]`
f0: `[0.2741742, 0.16532999, 0.12927616, 0.12932923, 0.2058461, 0.20574346, -0.029332343, -0.017752307, -0.017738394, -0.022428103, -0.022447986]`
